title: Services, EndpointSlices, DNS, Discovery, and Traffic Policies · API Object Lens: K8s Traffic Session Locality Policy
summary: A frontend loses backends as Pods rotate because it pins Pod IPs; a Service selector overlaps a canary and traffic policies change source and locality behavior. Planned artifact: Add and verify the k8s traffic session locality policy increment in service discovery and endpoint evidence map. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Evaluate session affinity, internal and external traffic policies, source preservation, locality, health checks, uneven load, and failure-domain tradeoffs.
Resolve namespace-qualified Service and Pod records, search paths, headless answers, readiness publication, caching, and application retry behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Predict K8s Traffic Session Locality Policy · Theory K8s Traffic Session Locality Policy
steps.instruction:

Trace selector, readiness, virtual IP, EndpointSlice, port mapping, DNS, and session behavior. Competency probe: Evaluate session affinity, internal and external traffic policies, source preservation, locality, health checks, uneven load, and failure-domain tradeoffs.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example Service selector matches the wrong Pods, EndpointSlices show no ready endpoints, a client calls localhost, and DNS search expansion reaches an unintended namespace. Trace selector, readiness, virtual IP, EndpointSlice, port mapping, DNS, and session behavior. Competency probe: Evaluate session affinity, internal and external traffic policies, source preservation, locality, health checks, uneven load, and failure-domain tradeoffs. A defensible solution applies K8s Traffic Session Locality Policy, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to service discovery and endpoint evidence map.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-worked-k8s-services-endpoints-dns-3
  - competency: k8s-worked-k8s-services-endpoints-dns-3
    artifact: service-discovery.yaml
    decision: worked k8s services endpoints dns 3
    environment: shared multi-tenant cluster under Restricted Pod Security
    fault-injection: drop one metrics series during an autoscaling surge
    retained-proof: accessible incident timeline with causal regression evidence
    case-key: worked3
    change: |
      apiVersion: v1
      kind: Service
      metadata:
      name: status-api
      spec:
      type: ClusterIP
      selector: {app: status-api}
      endpoint-evidence: ready-serving-terminating
      dns: status-api.intake.svc.cluster.local
    verify: kubectl get service,endpointslice -l kubernetes.io/service-name=status-api -o json
    failure: Reject Pod IP pinning, short-name cross-namespace assumptions, Service-as-health-probe claims, or locality policy as automatic availability.
    repair: Align selectors, inspect EndpointSlice conditions, use qualified DNS, test bind and route layers, and document platform-specific exposure.
content.caption: Runnable Kubernetes object and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

# Evidence: k8s-traffic-session-locality-policy-1w9wn8
  - competency: k8s-traffic-session-locality-policy
    artifact: service-discovery.yaml
    decision: traffic session locality policy
    environment: single-node minikube profile with a constrained Docker driver
    fault-injection: delete one ready Pod during a configuration rollout
    retained-proof: component versions skew drain timing and recovery rehearsal
    case-key: 1w9wn8
    change: |
      apiVersion: v1
      kind: Service
      metadata:
      name: status-api
      spec:
      type: ClusterIP
      selector: {app: status-api}
      endpoint-evidence: ready-serving-terminating
      dns: status-api.intake.svc.cluster.local
    verify: kubectl get service,endpointslice -l kubernetes.io/service-name=status-api -o json
    failure: Reject Pod IP pinning, short-name cross-namespace assumptions, Service-as-health-probe claims, or locality policy as automatic availability.
    repair: Align selectors, inspect EndpointSlice conditions, use qualified DNS, test bind and route layers, and document platform-specific exposure.
